title: 'Suspicious item' found at RAF Lossiemouth as site locked down by police

sections.heading: What happened
sections.content: Surrounding areas and roads have been closed off as a result of the ongoing incident.

sections.heading: Key details
sections.content: 'Suspicious item' found at RAF Lossiemouth as site locked down by police Police raced to the base at at RAF Lossiemouth in Moray (Image: PA)

sections.heading: Why it matters
sections.content: A Scottish RAF base has been locked down after reports of the discovery of a suspicious package. Bomb squads raced to the base at RAF Lossiemouth in Moray after receiving the alarm at 11.25am on Thursday, March 12. Surrounding areas and roads have been cordoned off by police as a result of the ongoing incident. Residents in nearby homes have also been evacuated as a precaution.
